evidence: HttpApiUtils centralises all HTTP boundary concerns (JSON serialisation, CORS headers, error codes, parameter validation) — no ad-hoc response building leaks into handlers
RelationUtils.resolveRelationAlias isolates alias→canonical-ID translation in one static map; all handlers call through it uniformly

CollocateQueryHelper is a package-private helper that extracts grouped collocate search + scoring shared by three BlackLabQueryExecutor methods; the CollocateSearch record makes the handoff data explicit
ExplorationHandlers.parseExploreParams and resolveRelationConfig are shared preamble methods reused across both exploration handlers, preventing duplicated parsing/error logic
QueryExecutor interface provides a clean seam between the HTTP layer and the index layer; ExplorationHandlers only sees SemanticFieldExplorer, never the raw executor

In exploreMultiSeed (lines 407-410 of SemanticFieldExplorer), ExplorationResult is constructed with seed=String.join(',', seeds) and discoveredNouns holding the seeds themselves — semantically different from single-seed mode where discoveredNouns are actually discovered similar nouns

ExplorationResult.getEdges() generates edge-type strings 'seed_adj' and 'discovered_adj' based on single-seed semantics; in multi-seed mode these labels are misleading because every 'discovered' noun is actually a seed

Both handleSemanticFieldExploreMulti (lines 98-114) and handleSemanticFieldComparison (lines 148-152) in ExplorationHandlers contain identical comma-split/trim/toLowerCase/LinkedHashSet seed-cleaning logic with no extraction

BlackLabSnippetParser.extractLemmaAt (line 220-222) is a one-line forwarding alias for extractCollocateFromXmlByPosition with no added behaviour — the 'Consolidated clean API' comment implies this was meant to unify callers, but CollocateQueryHelper still calls extractCollocateFromXmlByPosition directly

WordSketchApiServer constructor creates SemanticFieldExplorer, SketchHandlers, and ExplorationHandlers internally (lines 46-48) rather than accepting them; a TODO comment at line 49 acknowledges this is wrong but defers it — the server boundary absorbs construction responsibilities instead of delegating them
SemanticFieldExplorerTest covers only compareCollocateProfiles(); the two primary exploration paths exploreByPattern() and exploreMultiSeed() (412-line class) have zero stub-based unit test coverage
CollocateQueryHelper (240+ lines, two-phase BCQL collect→score pipeline, core ranking logic) has no test class at all

All four live-index test classes (BlackLabQueryExecutorTest, DependencySketchTest, BlackLabBcqlConcordanceTest, BlackLabDummyTest) guard with Assumptions.assumeTrue(indexDir.exists()); they permanently skip in any CI environment without the D:\corpora_philsci\bi path
CqlUtils (pure static functions, fully testable in isolation) has no tests despite PatternSubstitution — a sibling pure-utility class — being well covered
BlackLabSnippetParserTest is thorough: 300 lines, covers null/empty/boundary/multi-token cases for every public method
HandlersTest uses a well-crafted MockExchange and covers both 400 error paths and 200 happy paths for SketchHandlers and ExplorationHandlers
SemanticFieldExplorerTest employs a clean StubExecutor pattern, covering intersection/partial-sharing/edge-weight logic without requiring a real index
Class names are precise and domain-specific throughout: BlackLabQueryExecutor, CollocateQueryHelper, SemanticFieldExplorer, BlackLabSnippetParser — no vague generic names
Domain model records AdjectiveProfile, DiscoveredNoun, CoreCollocate, ExploreOptions, ExplorationResult all name the concept, not the structure
Handler methods use clear action verbs: handleSemanticFieldExplore, handleSemanticFieldExploreMulti, handleSemanticFieldComparison, handleConcordanceExamples
ExplorationHandlers.buildExploreResponseBody line 316 uses 'ca' for CoreCollocate; line 309 uses 'dn' for DiscoveredNoun — opaque abbreviations not established elsewhere in the codebase

BlackLabQueryExecutor.findCollocations (line 66) names its POS map 'posMap', while executeDependencyPattern (line 166) names the same concept 'lemmaPosMap' for a structurally identical Map<String,String>

Four private format methods in ExplorationHandlers (formatSeedCollocate, formatDiscoveredNoun, formatCoreCollocate, formatEdge) all declare the return map as 'Map<String,Object> m', below codebase norms — contrast with formatAdjectiveProfile which uses 'adjMap' and 'scores'
HttpApiUtils.requireParam line 95: local variable named 'v' for a parameter value — fine scope but inconsistent with codebase convention that prefers full names
buildBaseExploreResponse uses 'paramsUsed' for a map that becomes the 'parameters' JSON key in the response — the variable name implies what produced it, not what it represents
DiscoveredNoun record has both cumulativeScore (raw sum of logDice) and similarityScore (sharedCount * avgLogDice) — the distinction between these two scoring concepts is not clear from names alone

SemanticFieldExplorer.exploreByPattern(String seed, RelationConfig, ExploreOptions) bundles tuning params into ExploreOptions, but the sibling method exploreMultiSeed(Set<String> seeds, RelationConfig, double minLogDice, int topCollocates, int minShared) takes the same logical parameters as 3 flat args instead

QueryExecutor interface mixes result-type families and threshold responsibility: findCollocations/executeSurfacePattern/executeDependencyPattern all accept minLogDice and maxResults and return WordSketchResult, while executeQuery/executeBcqlQuery drop minLogDice entirely and return ConcordanceResult/CollocateResult — the split is justified by purpose but the naming (findCollocations vs executeQuery vs executeBcqlQuery) obscures which methods threshold results internally

HttpApiUtils methods are fully consistent: sendJsonResponse(exchange, data), sendError(exchange, code, message), sendOptionsResponse(exchange, allowedMethods), sendBinaryResponse(exchange, contentType, bytes) — uniform exchange-first convention throughout

All ExplorationHandlers handler methods follow the same shape: parse query string → requireParam null-check → resolveRelationConfig → parseExploreParams → call explorer → build response Map → sendJsonResponse; error paths are uniformly handled via sendError

compareCollocateProfiles(Set<String> seeds, double minLogDice, int topCollocates) uses flat params — same flat-param style as exploreMultiSeed, but ExploreOptions bundles an overlapping set (topCollocates, minLogDice, minShared) for exploreByPattern

strengths: HttpApiUtils is a single, explicit translation layer for all HTTP response concerns — CORS, serialisation, method validation, parameter validation — so no handler duplicates this logic
RelationUtils.resolveRelationAlias puts alias resolution in one obvious place; every handler calls through it and the result is always lower-cased and canonical
CollocateQueryHelper's CollocateSearch record makes the query→ranking handoff explicit: headwordFreq and HitGroups travel together as a named tuple rather than out-parameters
ExplorationHandlers.parseExploreParams and resolveRelationConfig avoid copy-pasting the shared request-parsing preamble across two nearly-identical handlers
The QueryExecutor interface creates a clean, well-documented seam so that ExplorationHandlers and SketchHandlers never import BlackLab types — index coupling is contained
mid_level_elegance.issue_character: Issues are localised but recurrent boundary shortcuts: the same model class is semantically repurposed across two exploration modes without adapting its edge-generation contract, seed-string parsing is duplicated rather than extracted, and the API server's constructor absorbs object construction that should be injected — each a small seam that adds cognitive overhead when tracing data flow.
mid_level_elegance.score_rationale: The codebase sits solidly in the 'decent foundations with identifiable friction' band. The utility layer (HttpApiUtils, RelationUtils, CollocateQueryHelper) is genuinely well-designed — inputs/outputs at each boundary are explicit and data translation is localised. What pulls the score down is the ExplorationResult semantic overloading across single/multi-seed modes: the same model, edge-type strings, and response builder are shared, hiding a meaningful difference in what 'discoveredNouns' means and producing subtly wrong edge labels in multi-seed output. Alongside the duplicated seed-parsing boilerplate, the constructor-owned collaborators in WordSketchApiServer, and a dead-alias method in BlackLabSnippetParser, the codebase reads as mostly clean with a recurring pattern of boundary shortcuts rather than deep structural dysfunction — placing it in the 65-70 range rather than below 60.

strengths: BlackLabSnippetParserTest is exemplary: all public methods covered with null, empty, boundary, multi-token, and fallback cases — a genuine high-quality unit test suite
SemanticFieldExplorerTest introduces a clean StubExecutor that isolates the comparison/ranking logic from the BlackLab backend, demonstrating the right test architecture for this codebase
HandlersTest uses a compact MockExchange to test both input-validation 400 paths and full 200 happy-paths for HTTP handlers without starting a server
RelationUtilsTest exhaustively covers case-insensitivity and alias resolution, including null input and unknown input passthrough
test_strategy.issue_character: The gaps are concentrated in two layers that are never reached by the existing stub-based tests: the outer exploration orchestration (SemanticFieldExplorer's exploreByPattern/exploreMultiSeed paths) and the inner execution backbone (CollocateQueryHelper), while every live-index test that could close those gaps is permanently skipped in CI.
test_strategy.score_rationale: The codebase scores above 40 because the parsing and utility layers are well-tested and the test infrastructure (stubs, MockExchange, GrammarConfigHelper) is thoughtfully designed. However, the two most critical runtime paths — the product's core exploration feature (exploreByPattern/exploreMultiSeed) and the collocate ranking pipeline (CollocateQueryHelper) — have zero automated coverage. All four live-index tests that would verify the BlackLab integration are structurally uncallable in CI due to hard-coded local paths. This places the codebase firmly in the 'significant drag' band: solid testing culture in the utility layer but the business logic core is untested.

strengths: Class and interface names throughout are precise domain terms with no vague verbs: SemanticFieldExplorer, CollocateQueryHelper, BlackLabSnippetParser, AdjectiveCollocateComparator

Domain model records consistently name the concept, not the container: AdjectiveProfile, DiscoveredNoun, CoreCollocate, ExploreOptions — a developer unfamiliar with the codebase can infer purpose immediately
API handler method names follow a clear and consistent handle* pattern with specific subject+action naming (handleSemanticFieldExploreMulti, handleConcordanceExamples)
The logDice domain term is applied consistently throughout field names, parameters, constants, and JSON keys with no divergence
RelationUtils.resolveRelationAlias, buildAndRankCollocates, buildRawAdjectiveProfiles, buildBaseExploreResponse all reveal intent precisely
naming_quality.issue_character: Issues are scattered and localized rather than systemic: a cluster of single-letter local variable names in short formatter methods, one vocabulary divergence between two methods naming the same map concept differently, and one ambiguous dual-score field in a domain model record.
naming_quality.score_rationale: The codebase names things well at every structural level — packages, classes, interfaces, public methods, and domain model fields are all precise and communicative. What pulls the score down from the 85+ range is a recurring pattern of opaque abbreviations in private formatter methods (m, ca, dn, v) that are inconsistent with the fuller names used everywhere else, plus one genuine vocabulary divergence (posMap vs lemmaPosMap) for the same concept in the same file, and one unclear dual-score naming in DiscoveredNoun. These are real drag on a developer reading the format internals but do not affect the dominant naming quality of the codebase, placing it solidly in the 78-80 range — strong naming with uneven spots in a specific layer.

strengths: HttpApiUtils is a model of API consistency: all response-sending methods take HttpExchange as their first argument with predictable subsequent parameters, and the class is exclusively static utilities with no state

QueryExecutor is thoroughly documented with Javadoc that explicitly distinguishes the four execution styles (CQL vs BCQL, concordance vs collocate), reducing the cognitive cost of the naming asymmetry

All handler methods in ExplorationHandlers share a uniform control-flow skeleton (requireParam → resolveRelationConfig → parseExploreParams → service call → sendJsonResponse), making the error-path shape predictable across endpoints
ExploreOptions exists as a parameter-object for exploreByPattern, demonstrating that the team recognized the value of bundling related tuning knobs
api_surface_coherence.issue_character: The core issue is intra-class inconsistency within SemanticFieldExplorer: the ExploreOptions parameter-object pattern exists and is used for one public method but not extended to sibling methods that accept overlapping parameters as flat args.
api_surface_coherence.score_rationale: The HTTP-layer API (HttpApiUtils, handler methods) is consistently shaped and the QueryExecutor interface is well-annotated, which pushes quality above 60. However, the public SemanticFieldExplorer API is split: exploreByPattern uses ExploreOptions while exploreMultiSeed and compareCollocateProfiles take the same logical parameters as flat args, creating inconsistency at the most-used service-layer boundary. This is not a deeply structural problem — it is fixable with an overload — but a developer navigating between these methods will notice the mismatch and cannot apply the same call pattern. Against global anchors, this is solidly 'significant drag that is not ubiquitous,' placing the score around 70.

issues.summary: ExplorationResult reused across single-seed and multi-seed modes with different semantics for discoveredNouns, producing wrong edge-type labels in multi-seed output
issues.suggestion: Introduce a separate result type (e.g. MultiSeedExplorationResult) or a factory method that builds the edge list correctly for multi-seed context (edge type 'seed_collocate' instead of 'discovered_adj'), keeping the API response shape consistent while making the semantic difference explicit at the boundary rather than hiding it inside getEdges()
evidence: exploreMultiSeed (line 392) stores each seed word as a DiscoveredNoun with seed's own collocates — semantically the seeds are not 'discovered' nouns
ExplorationResult is constructed with seed=String.join(',', seeds) (line 408), so getEdges() generates a 'seed_adj' edge from a comma-joined multi-seed string rather than a real seed word
ExplorationResult.getEdges() emits type='discovered_adj' for edges from discoveredNouns, but in multi-seed context these nodes are the input seeds, not discovered similar words
buildExploreResponseBody in ExplorationHandlers calls result.getEdges() identically for both modes, so the API returns misleadingly-labelled edge types in multi-seed responses

issues.summary: Identical comma-split/trim/toLowerCase/LinkedHashSet seed-cleaning logic duplicated in two ExplorationHandlers methods
issues.suggestion: Extract a private parseSeeds(String csv, int minCount) method that performs the split/trim/lowercase/dedup and throws a validated error or returns the set, then call it from both handlers
evidence: handleSemanticFieldExploreMulti lines 98-114: splits seedsStr by comma, trims, lowercases, adds to LinkedHashSet, rejects size < 2
handleSemanticFieldComparison lines 148-152: identical split/trim/lowercase/LinkedHashSet logic on nounsParam
The two blocks are functionally identical but separated by ~50 lines and use different variable names (seedsStr vs nounsParam)

issues.summary: WordSketchApiServer constructor constructs SemanticFieldExplorer and handler objects instead of accepting them, making the API boundary opaque
issues.suggestion: Accept SketchHandlers and ExplorationHandlers as constructor parameters, constructing them at the call site in Main.java; this makes the composition graph visible at the wiring layer and removes construction from the server boundary
evidence: Constructor at line 46 creates 'new SemanticFieldExplorer(executor)' rather than accepting it as a parameter
Lines 47-48 create SketchHandlers and ExplorationHandlers internally from the constructed components
A TODO comment at line 49 acknowledges server setup responsibilities should move out of the constructor but defers it

issues.summary: BlackLabSnippetParser.extractLemmaAt is a trivial one-line alias for extractCollocateFromXmlByPosition with no added behaviour, creating unnecessary indirection at the module boundary
issues.suggestion: Inline extractCollocateFromXmlByPosition into extractLemmaAt (make it the single implementation), or remove extractLemmaAt and update callers to call extractCollocateFromXmlByPosition directly; either choice eliminates the ambiguous alias
evidence: extractLemmaAt (line 220-222) calls extractCollocateFromXmlByPosition(xml, position) and returns the result unchanged

The 'Consolidated clean API' comment implies extractLemmaAt should be the canonical entry point, but CollocateQueryHelper.extractCollocateLemma (line 282) still calls extractCollocateFromXmlByPosition directly, bypassing the alias
Two names for the same operation with no behavioural distinction forces readers to verify they are equivalent before trusting either

issues.summary: SemanticFieldExplorer.exploreByPattern() and exploreMultiSeed() have zero unit coverage despite being the application's primary feature paths
issues.suggestion: Add stub-based tests for exploreByPattern() and exploreMultiSeed() using the existing StubExecutor pattern: verify that ExplorationResult contains expected discovered_nouns, core_collocates, and edges for a controlled collocate map
evidence: SemanticFieldExplorerTest covers only compareCollocateProfiles(); no test exercises exploreByPattern(seed, relationConfig, opts) or exploreMultiSeed()

SemanticFieldExplorer is 412 lines with non-trivial private helpers (fetchSeedCollocates, buildNounCollocateMap, scoreAndFilterCandidates, identifyCoreCollocates) — all dead code from the test perspective
The StubExecutor in SemanticFieldExplorerTest already supports findCollocations() and executeSurfacePattern(), so coverage could be added without new infrastructure

issues.summary: CollocateQueryHelper — the two-phase BCQL collect→score pipeline that all collocate ranking depends on — has no test coverage at all
issues.suggestion: Create CollocateQueryHelperTest covering: (1) extractCollocateLemma with pos=0 vs pos>0 inputs, (2) buildAndRankCollocates with a mock getTotalFrequency by extracting a testable interface or using a subclass stub for the frequency lookup
evidence: CollocateQueryHelper is 240+ lines with package-private methods collectHits(), scoreHits(), buildAndRankCollocates(), and the static extractCollocateLemma()
The static extractCollocateLemma() method is pure logic (delegates to BlackLabSnippetParser based on collocatePos) and is directly testable without a BlackLab index
buildAndRankCollocates() applies logDice filtering and sorting — the ranking correctness is never verified in isolation
No test file exists for CollocateQueryHelper; grep across src/test confirms zero references

issues.summary: All four live-index test classes permanently skip in CI due to hard-coded local index paths, providing no integration safety net
issues.suggestion: Ship a minimal test index (50-100 sentence CoNLL-U fixture) under src/test/resources/ and use it in these tests so the BlackLab integration is verified on every CI run; keep the env-var override for developer convenience with real corpora
evidence: All four classes default to 'D:\\corpora_philsci\\bi' when CONCEPT_SKETCH_TEST_INDEX env var is absent — a Windows-only local path that will never exist in CI
BlackLabDummyTest iterates patterns and calls assumeTrue(false) on IOException, meaning the test can pass vacuously even if every pattern fails
BlackLabBcqlConcordanceTest uses @EnabledIf("indexExists") which evaluates to false in CI, silently suppressing 2 tests

issues.summary: CqlUtils — pure static utility used by both PatternSubstitution and GrammarConfigLoader — has no tests despite being trivially testable
issues.suggestion: Add CqlUtilsTest with cases for extractConstraintAttribute (found, not found, null input, multiple attributes) and the escape helper (backslash, quote, combined)
evidence: CqlUtils contains extractConstraintAttribute() and CQL-escaping helpers — pure string processing with no dependencies
PatternSubstitution (a sibling utility) has 95-line test coverage, showing the pattern is established
No reference to CqlUtils appears anywhere in src/test/

issues.summary: Same concept named 'posMap' in findCollocations and 'lemmaPosMap' in executeDependencyPattern within the same file
issues.suggestion: Standardise on one name for this concept across both methods; 'collocatePosMap' makes the key domain explicit — rename line 66's 'posMap' to 'collocatePosMap' to match line 166's more descriptive style (or vice-versa)
evidence: Line 66: Map<String, String> posMap = new HashMap<>(); — holds collocate-lemma → POS-tag mapping
Line 166: Map<String, String> lemmaPosMap = new HashMap<>(); — identical concept and usage in executeDependencyPattern
Both maps are passed to helper.buildAndRankCollocates(freqMap, posMap/lemmaPosMap, ...) in the same role

issues.summary: Four private format methods in ExplorationHandlers all use 'm' as the local map variable, inconsistent with the codebase norm of descriptive local names
issues.suggestion: Rename 'm' to a name matching the method's subject: 'collocateMap' in formatSeedCollocate, 'nounMap' in formatDiscoveredNoun, 'coreCollocateMap' in formatCoreCollocate, 'edgeMap' in formatEdge
evidence: formatSeedCollocate line 258: Map<String, Object> m = new HashMap<>();
formatDiscoveredNoun line 266: Map<String, Object> m = new HashMap<>();
formatCoreCollocate line 276: Map<String, Object> m = new HashMap<>();
formatEdge line 286: Map<String, Object> m = new HashMap<>();
Contrast: formatAdjectiveProfile (line 371) uses 'adjMap' and 'scores' — clearly descriptive names in the same class

issues.summary: Loop variables 'ca' (CoreCollocate) and 'dn' (DiscoveredNoun) in ExplorationHandlers are unexplained abbreviations not established in codebase convention
issues.suggestion: Expand to 'discoveredNoun' / 'noun' and 'coreCollocate' / 'collocate' respectively; the full names are not verbose in this context
evidence: Line 309: for (DiscoveredNoun dn : result.getDiscoveredNouns())
Line 316: for (CoreCollocate ca : result.getCoreCollocates())
Line 265 parameter: formatDiscoveredNoun(DiscoveredNoun dn) — also uses the abbreviation in the method signature
Line 275: formatCoreCollocate(CoreCollocate ca) — abbreviation in parameter name
No other place in the codebase uses 'dn' or 'ca' as type abbreviations

issues.summary: DiscoveredNoun has both 'cumulativeScore' and 'similarityScore' fields where the distinction between the two scoring concepts is not apparent from names alone
issues.suggestion: Rename 'cumulativeScore' to 'totalLogDiceSum' or 'sumLogDice' to make its computation explicit; rename 'similarityScore' to 'weightedSimilarity' or 'rankedScore' to distinguish it as the primary ranking key
evidence: DiscoveredNoun record fields: double cumulativeScore and double similarityScore
SemanticFieldExplorer line 249: double cumulativeScore = collocScores.values().stream().mapToDouble(Double::doubleValue).sum(); — it is a raw sum
SemanticFieldExplorer line 252: new DiscoveredNoun(..., cumulativeScore, avgLogDice, sharedCount * avgLogDice) — similarityScore is sharedCount * avgLogDice
Both fields are conceptually 'how similar is this noun to the seed' and diverge only in formula; neither name communicates the formula

issues.summary: exploreMultiSeed takes flat minLogDice/topCollocates/minShared args while sibling exploreByPattern uses ExploreOptions for the same parameters
issues.suggestion: Add an overload exploreMultiSeed(Set<String> seeds, RelationConfig, ExploreOptions) that delegates to the current flat-param implementation, and do the same for compareCollocateProfiles; update ExplorationHandlers to call the options-taking overloads so the service layer sees a uniform shape
evidence: exploreByPattern(String seed, RelationConfig, ExploreOptions opts) bundles topCollocates, minLogDice, minShared into ExploreOptions
exploreMultiSeed(Set<String> seeds, RelationConfig relationConfig, double minLogDice, int topCollocates, int minShared) spreads the same logical parameters as positional arguments
compareCollocateProfiles(Set<String> seeds, double minLogDice, int topCollocates) also uses flat params, reinforcing the split
